Scenario.title: Operation Dream Job Water and Wastewater Systems Attack Scenario
Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Complex Operation Dream Job attackers target water and wastewater systems with sophisticated attacks, exploiting vulnerabilities in employee devices and networks to disrupt operations.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: ["Device theft", "Weak passwords", "Improper configuration", "Vulnerability", "Internet-facing assets", "Shared DB or directory", "Obsolete products", "Injection flaws", "Custom APIs", "Autonomous system numbers(ASNs)", "SSL Certificates and Attribution", "WHOIS records, contacts and history", "TCP/IP ports", "Public and private cloud", "Web server services such as emails and databases", "Shadow IT", "Poor implementation of access controls", "Malicious insiders", "Social engineering", "Insufficient physical security controls", "Authentication issues"]
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: ["External/Removable media", "Loss or Theft of equipment", "Attrition", "Web", "Account takeover", "Inadequate encryption", "Browser-based attack", "Application compromise", "Open port", "Vulnerability exploit", "Ransomware", "Malware", "Man in The Middle(MiTM)", "Session hijacking", "Brute-force attack", "Distributed Denial of Service(DDoS)", "Watering-hole", "Compromised and weak credentials", "SIM Swap", "Security misconfigurations and vulnerabilities", "Web forms", "IP address and IP blocks", "Domains and subdomains", "Botnet", "Credential compromise", "Telephony Denial of Service(TDoS)", "Replay Attack", "Cryptanalysis Attacks", "Side Channel Attacks", "Hardware Trojans", "Covert Attacks", "Sleep Deprivation Attack", "Clock Skewing", "Attacks Targeting System Reputation", "Data and Command Integrity Attack", "Eavesdropping", "Command and Control Attacks", "Phishing", "Impersonations/Spoofing", "Improper usage", "Dumpster diving", "Advanced Persistent Threat(APT)", "Baiting", "Shoulder surfing", "Reverse social engineering", "Business Email Compromise(BEC)", "Advanced Fee", "Extortion"]

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.technique_name: Spear Phishing via Compromised Email Account
stage.description: The attackers send spear-phishing emails to specific employees, tricking them into revealing their login credentials.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers exploit vulnerabilities in employee devices to gain initial access to the system.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attackers run malicious code on compromised systems to achieve their objectives.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attackers inject malware into running processes to maintain persistence.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attackers schedule tasks to maintain persistence on the system.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attackers modify the boot process to maintain persistence on the system.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attackers use elevated privileges to access sensitive areas of the system.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attackers exploit vulnerabilities to gain elevated privileges.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: The attackers evade detection by using anti-debugging techniques.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: The attackers obfuscate malware to avoid detection.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attackers attempt to guess login credentials.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attackers capture login credentials using a keylogger.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attackers identify network services and open ports.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attackers gather system information.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: The attackers move laterally through remote services.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: The attackers use valid accounts to move laterally.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attackers collect data from local systems.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attackers collect data from network traffic.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attackers exfiltrate data over alternative protocols.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attackers exfiltrate data using FTP.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers disrupt water and wastewater systems.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers destroy critical data.

Scenario.summary: Operation Dream Job attackers employ complex tactics to disrupt water and wastewater systems, including spear phishing, drive-by compromise, code execution, and data destruction.
